```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=8}
    plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce1, colour_by="TSCAN.2", point_size = .3, text_by="label", text_colour="red") + ggline,
    plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce1, colour_by="TSCAN.8", point_size = .3, text_by="label", text_colour="red") + ggline,
    ncol=2
)
```

### In batch corrected space 

It's hard to interpret this in TSNE space, so we'll plot in batch corrected expression space instead because the clusters and trajectory analysis were actually calculated in batch corrected expression space. 

```{r, fig.height=4, fig.width=8}
line.data.sub_corrected = line.data_corrected[grepl("^1--", line.data_corrected$edge) | grepl("--1$", line.data_corrected$edge),]
line.data.sub_corrected <- line.data.sub_corrected[grepl("3--", line.data.sub_corrected$edge) | grepl("4--", line.data.sub_corrected$edge),]

ggline_corrected <- geom_line(data=line.data.sub_corrected, mapping=aes(x=dim1, y=dim2, group=edge))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(
    plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce1, dimred = "corrected", colour_by="TSCAN.2", point_size = .5, text_by="label", text_colour="red") + ggline_corrected,
    plotTSNE(merged.sex.sce1, dimred = "corrected", colour_by="TSCAN.8", point_size = .5, text_by="label", text_colour="red") + ggline_corrected,
    ncol=2
)
```

```{r}
pseudo_test2 <- testPseudotime(combined.sex.sce1, df=1, pseudotime=combined.sex.sce1$TSCAN.2)
pseudo_test2$SYMBOL <- rowData(combined.sex.sce1)$SYMBOL
#pseudo_test2[order(pseudo_test2$p.value),]
```

```{r}
pseudo_test8 <- testPseudotime(combined.sex.sce1, df=1, pseudotime=combined.sex.sce1$TSCAN.8)
pseudo_test8$SYMBOL <- rowData(combined.sex.sce1)$SYMBOL
#pseudo_test4[order(pseudo_test4$p.value),]
```

We want to find genes that are significant in path 2 and are not significant and/or changing in the opposite direction in path 8 and vise versa. We use the raw p-values to look for non-significant genes in order to increase the stringency of the definition of unique genes in our path.

## Genes uniquely changing on path 2 vs 8

These are saved in `results/filtered_sex_only2_DEgenes.csv`.

```{r}
only2 <- pseudo_test2[which(pseudo_test2$FDR <= 0.05 & 
    (pseudo_test8$p.value >= 0.05 | sign(pseudo_test2$logFC)!=sign(pseudo_test8$logFC))),]

only2 = as.data.frame(only2) 
only2$Gene = rownames(only2) 
only2 = only2[c(4,1,2,3)]

write.csv(only2, file = paste0(results_dir, "/filtered_sex_only2_DEgenes.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(only2[order(only2$p.value),], filter="top", rownames = FALSE)
```

### Top 50 heatmap

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=6}
on.path2 <- !is.na(combined.sex.sce1$TSCAN.2)

top50 =
  only2 %>%
  slice_min(order_by = FDR, n=50) %>%
  pull(Gene)

plotHeatmap(combined.sex.sce1[,on.path2], 
            order_columns_by="TSCAN.2", 
            colour_columns_by="TSCAN.2", 
            features=top50,
            center=TRUE)
```

### Same genes on path 8. 

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=6}
on.path8 <- !is.na(combined.sex.sce1$TSCAN.8)

plotHeatmap(combined.sex.sce1[,on.path8], 
            order_columns_by="TSCAN.8", 
            colour_columns_by="TSCAN.8", 
            features=top50,
            center=TRUE)
```

## Gene ontology enrichment of genes uniquely changing on path 2 vs 8

**Columns**

* `GOID`: Gene ontology ID            
* `GOName`: Gene ontology name    
* `nGOTermGenes`: Number of genes assigned to GO term        
* `Path`: Path and whether the gene is up or down regulated      
* `nPathDEGenes`: Number of genes differentially expressed on the path         
* `nSharedGenes`: Number of shared genes between the GO term and path DE genes   
* `Pval`: p-value          
* `FDR`: p-value corrected for multiple comparisons            
          
These results are save in `results/filtered_sex_only2_gene_GO_pvals.csv`. Overlapping genes are save in `results/filtered_sex_only2_gene_GO_overlaps.csv`

```{r}
gaf = read.delim("/mnt/research/bioinformaticsCore/shared/Genomes/Petromyzon_marinus/GCF_010993605.1_kPetMar1.pri_gene_ontology.gaf", header=F, comment = "!")

gaf <- 
  gaf %>%
  filter(!grepl("NOT", V4)) %>%  
  dplyr::select(V3, V5) %>%      
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "V5",            
                Gene = "V3") %>%
  distinct() 

keep_go = 
  gaf %>%
  group_by(GOID) %>%
  tally() %>%
  filter(n > 10,
         n < 200) %>%
  pull(GOID)

gaf = 
  gaf %>%
  filter(GOID %in% keep_go)

go_names = read.delim("/mnt/research/bioinformaticsCore/shared/Genomes/Petromyzon_marinus/GOs_names.txt", header = F)
colnames(go_names) = c("GOID", "GOName")

only2_genes = 
  only2 %>%
  mutate(Path = case_when(
    logFC > 0 ~ "Only2.Upregulated",
    logFC < 0 ~  "Only2.Downregulated")
    ) %>%
  select(Gene, Path)
  
  
go_ol =  phyperOverlapSets(gaf, 
                           only2_genes, length(merged.sce))

write.csv(go_ol$intersecting.genes, file = paste0(results_dir, "/filtered_sex_only2_gene_GO_overlaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

scores = go_ol$pval.scores

scores = 
  scores %>%
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "Group1",
                Path = "Group2",
                nGOTermGenes = "nGroup1",
                nPathDEGenes = "nGroup2") %>%
    mutate(FDR = p.adjust(Pval, "BH")) %>%
  select(-Enrichment)

scores = 
  left_join(go_names, scores, by="GOID") %>%
  filter(!is.na(nGOTermGenes)) %>%
  filter(Pval < .01)

write.csv(scores, file = paste0(results_dir, "/filtered_sex_only2_gene_GO_pvals.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(scores, filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```

## Genes uniquely changing on path 8 vs 2

These are saved in `results/filtered_sex_only8_DEgenes.csv`.

```{r}
only8 <- pseudo_test8[which(pseudo_test8$FDR <= 0.05 & 
    (pseudo_test2$p.value >= 0.05 | sign(pseudo_test2$logFC)!=sign(pseudo_test8$logFC))),]

only8 = as.data.frame(only8) 
only8$Gene = rownames(only8) 
only8 = only8[c(4,1,2,3)]

write.csv(only8, file = paste0(results_dir, "/filtered_sex_only8_DEgenes.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(only8[order(only8$p.value),], filter="top", rownames = FALSE)
```

### Top 50 heatmap

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=6}
on.path8 <- !is.na(combined.sex.sce1$TSCAN.8)

top50 =
  only8 %>%
  slice_min(order_by = FDR, n=50) %>%
  pull(Gene)

plotHeatmap(combined.sex.sce1[,on.path8], 
            order_columns_by="TSCAN.8", 
            colour_columns_by="TSCAN.8", 
            features=top50,
            center=TRUE)
```

### Same genes on path 2. 

There should not be a discernible correlation with pseudotime.  

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=6}
plotHeatmap(combined.sex.sce1[,on.path2], 
            order_columns_by="TSCAN.2", 
            colour_columns_by="TSCAN.2", 
            features=top50,
            center=TRUE)
```

## Gene ontology enrichment of genes uniquely changing on path 8 vs 2

*Columns*

* `GOID`: Gene ontology ID            
* `GOName`: Gene ontology name    
* `nGOTermGenes`: Number of genes assigned to GO term        
* `Path`: Path and whether the gene is up or down regulated      
* `nPathDEGenes`: Number of genes differentially expressed on the path         
* `nSharedGenes`: Number of shared genes between the GO term and path DE genes   
* `Pval`: p-value          
* `FDR`: p-value corrected for multiple comparisons            

These results are save in `results/filtered_sex_only8_gene_GO_pvals.csv`. Overlapping genes are save in `results/filtered_sex_only8_gene_GO_overlaps.csv`

```{r}
only8_genes = 
  only8 %>%
  mutate(Path = case_when(
    logFC > 0 ~ "Only8.Upregulated",
    logFC < 0 ~  "Only8.Downregulated")
    ) %>%
  select(Gene, Path)
  
  
go_ol =  phyperOverlapSets(gaf, 
                           only8_genes, length(merged.sce))

write.csv(go_ol$intersecting.genes, file = paste0(results_dir, "/filtered_sex_only8_gene_GO_overlaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

scores = go_ol$pval.scores

scores = 
  scores %>%
  dplyr::rename(GOID = "Group1",
                Path = "Group2",
                nGOTermGenes = "nGroup1",
                nOnly8DEGenes = "nGroup2") %>%
    mutate(FDR = p.adjust(Pval, "BH")) %>%
  select(-Enrichment)

scores = 
  left_join(go_names, scores, by="GOID") %>%
  filter(!is.na(nGOTermGenes)) %>%
  filter(Pval < .01)

write.csv(scores, file = paste0(results_dir, "/filtered_sex_only8_gene_GO_pvals.csv"), row.names = FALSE) 

datatable(scores, filter = "top", rownames = FALSE)
```

# Session info
```{r}
sessionInfo()
```
